channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lateef Adedimeji and Mo Bimpe announce birth of triplet sons after years of public pressure
item.description: Popular Nollywood actor Lateef Adedimeji and his wife, actress Adebimpe Oyebade, also known as Mo Bimpe, have joyfully welcomed triplet sons. The announcement came on May 1, 2026, through an emotional...

item.title: Quillan Salkilld Eyes Top Contender Mateusz Gamrot After Convincing UFC Victory
item.description: Perth’s UFC rising star Quillan Salkilld is making waves in the lightweight division after a first-round TKO victory over veteran Beneil Dariush at RAC Arena. With a record of 12-1, the 26-year-old ha...

item.title: Opposition urges installation of CCTV cameras on Magufuli Bridge to improve road safety
item.description: Leaders from 13 opposition parties in Tanzania have called on the government, through Tanroads, to install CCTV cameras on the Magufuli Bridge to enhance safety for users. The bridge, completed at a c...

item.title: Nigeria Dominate African Wrestling Championships with Record 15th Women’s Title
item.description: Team Nigeria don show their supremacy again for African women wrestling as dem clinch the continental title for the 15th time at the African Wrestling Championships wey take place for Alexandria, Egyp...

item.title: Vũ Thảo My and BGYO's Gelo Share Optimism on Growing SEA-Pop Collaborations
item.description: Vietnamese singer Vũ Thảo My and BGYO member Gelo recently expressed their positive reactions to the increasing recognition of Southeast Asian pop music, or SEA-pop, in the regional and global music s...

item.title: Taiwan President Visits Eswatini Despite Chinese Opposition
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te arrived in Eswatini, formerly known as Swaziland, for an unannounced visit, affirming Taiwan's right to international engagement despite opposition from China. Beijing...

item.title: President Trump Shares 11 Posts on Truth Social During 42-Minute Late-Night Session
item.description: On Friday night, May 2, 2026, President Donald Trump engaged in a rapid posting session on Truth Social, sharing 11 posts within a 42-minute window between 11:03 p.m. and 11:45 p.m. The content ranged...

item.title: Lessons from Alberta for Canada’s Proposed Sovereign Wealth Fund
item.description: As Canada considers launching its own sovereign wealth fund, the federal government can draw important lessons from Alberta’s long-standing Heritage Savings Trust Fund. Alberta launched its fund in 19...

item.title: Pantami Suspends Social Media Aide Over Unauthorised Remarks About Opponents
item.description: Isa Pantami, former Minister of Communication and Digital Economy, wey dey eye Gombe State governorship for 2027, don suspend one of him social media aides indefinitely. This suspension na because the...

item.title: Ravenswood gold mine faces lender pressure after hedge providers issue default notices
item.description: Ravenswood, a Queensland-based gold mine owned by EMR Capital and Indonesia’s Widjaja family, is facing mounting financial and operational pressure as tensions escalate among its stakeholders. Accordi...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rain and Strong Winds in Several Counties Today
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather alert for several regions in the country today, Sunday, May 3. Residents in western and eastern Highlands, the Rift Valley, and parts of ...

item.title: Local elections may result in council seats being won with unusually low voter turnout
item.description: In the upcoming local elections, political analysts are warning that some council seats could be decided by historically low vote shares, potentially undermining the legitimacy of election results. Th...

item.title: NSFAS board appoints Waseem Carrim as CEO despite ministerial objections and resignations
item.description: A turbulent week at the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) culminated in the appointment of Waseem Carrim as the organisation’s permanent CEO, despite internal resignations and objections f...

item.title: Boston Blue Episode 17 Recap: Erin Returns and Lena Discovers Family Secrets
item.description: In Season 1, Episode 17 of 'Boston Blue,' titled 'L'dor Vador,' multiple storylines unfold that impact the Reagan and Silver families. Danny (Donnie Wahlberg) escorts his sister Erin (Bridget Moynahan...

item.title: Pengana to Launch IPO for AI-Focused Global Private Equity Trust
item.description: Pengana Capital Group is set to launch an initial public offering (IPO) for a new listed investment trust focused on unlisted companies, particularly those in the artificial intelligence sector. This ...

item.title: Trump reviews Iran's peace proposal amid continued tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has indicated he is reviewing a new 14-point proposal from Iran aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. While expressing scepticism over its acceptability, Trump suggested that...

item.title: Study warns extreme heat endangers health, jobs and food security in southern Africa
item.description: Extreme heat no longer be just uncomfortable weather for southern Africa, e don turn serious threat to health, jobs and food security, especially for poor people wey no get resources to cope. Unlike f...

item.title: U.S. weighs Iranian ceasefire proposal amid ongoing tensions and Strait of Hormuz standoff
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he is reviewing a new proposal from Iran aimed at ending the ongoing conflict, though he remains doubtful the terms will be acceptable. The proposal, reportedly consis...

item.title: Woman sentenced to life for killing her sister and stealing valuable watch in Camden
item.description: A 70-year-old woman, Nancy Pexton, has been sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 22 years for the murder of her sister, film director Jennifer Abbott, also known as Sarah Steinberg, a...

item.title: IEC ensures local elections will not disrupt school exams
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Africa (IEC) has confirmed that the upcoming local government elections on 4 November will not interfere with school activities, including the National Senior Certifi...
